title: NOC 92013 – Supervisors, plastic and rubber products manufacturing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92013 are the technical leaders of Canada's advanced materials manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the production of essential components for the automotive, aerospace, medical, and construction industries. In 2026, as Canada shifts toward a circular economy, these supervisors are at the forefront of integrating Bioplastics and Recycled Polymer processing into mainstream manufacturing. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP) and Express Entry Category-Based Selection.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9201 – Supervisors, processing and manufacturing occupationsNOC Code92013

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who operate plastic and rubber processing machines.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with other departments to ensure production targets are met.
Resolve work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product quality.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage equipment maintenance schedules to minimize downtime.
Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations and quality control standards (ISO 9001).
Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures, and the operation of new automated machinery.
Prepare production and safety reports for senior management and oversee waste reduction (Lean Manufacturing) initiatives.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific processing or manufacturing occupation supervised are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma in plastics technology, chemical technology, or mechanical engineering is increasingly preferred for modern automated plants.
Certifications: Internal company certifications and provincial safety designations (e.g., JHSC) are standard requirements.
Strong leadership, mechanical aptitude, and proficiency in Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, supervisors in specialized sectors like Medical Plastics or EV Battery Components often command higher premiums above the median wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the plastics and rubber sector will see a 10% growth in supervisory positions by 2033. The primary driver is the "Greening" of the manufacturing sector, which requires experienced supervisors to implement new material sciences and energy-efficient processing methods. Replacement demand due to the retirement of current supervisors in the Ontario automotive belt remains high.
labour_market_demand: The demand for plastic and rubber processing supervisors is Good to Very Good, especially in Canada’s industrial heartlands. In 2026, Southern Ontario (Windsor-Kitchener corridor) and Quebec are reporting a high need for supervisors to manage the production of lightweight plastic components for Electric Vehicles (EVs). Additionally, the medical device manufacturing sector in British Columbia is actively recruiting supervisory talent to manage high-precision cleanroom environments.
Advanced Manufacturing Hub: Work in a country that is a global leader in automotive parts and aerospace component manufacturing.
Sustainability Innovation: Lead the transition to sustainable manufacturing by managing advanced plastic recycling and biodegradable polymer production.
Technological Leadership: Manage "Industry 4.0" facilities utilizing automated injection molding, robotic assembly, and real-time data analytics.
Strategic PR Pathways: This occupation is highly valued in Express Entry draws for Trade and STEM occupations and specific provincial "In-Demand" lists.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade Occupations."
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based plastic or rubber manufacturer.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s high-tech manufacturing and medical device sectors.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable pathway for supervisors with the required work experience and a job offer.
